__comment: biblioHack — crawl & enrichment progress. Provisioned into the NAS grafana-stack (datasource uid bibliohack-pg, a read-only PostgreSQL datasource using the postgres_exporter 'metrics' user). Source of truth lives in the biblioHack repo at infra/grafana/.
title: biblioHack — crawl & enrichment

targets.rawSql: SELECT count(*) AS records FROM bibliographic_records

panels.title: TITN space covered
panels.description: scrape_tasks seen vs the probed TITN high-water mark (2,662,739)

targets.rawSql: SELECT round(100.0 * count(*) / 2662739, 2) AS pct FROM scrape_tasks

panels.title: Genre known
panels.description: Records with a CDU/tejuelo-derived genre (≠ unknown). Self-heals as re-scrapes run.

targets.rawSql: SELECT round(100.0 * count(*) FILTER (WHERE genre <> 'unknown') / greatest(count(*), 1), 1) AS pct FROM bibliographic_records

panels.title: Embeddings backfilled
panels.description: BGE-M3 vectors present (semantic/hybrid search quality). Backfill runs every 3h on the crawler plane.

targets.rawSql: SELECT round(100.0 * count(embedding) / greatest(count(*), 1), 1) AS pct FROM bibliographic_records
SELECT status, count(*) AS n FROM scrape_tasks GROUP BY status ORDER BY n DESC
SELECT date_trunc('day', first_seen_at) AS "time", count(*) AS "records" FROM bibliographic_records WHERE $__timeFilter(first_seen_at) GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Mirror size (cumulative)
panels.description: Whole history — ignores the dashboard time range.

targets.rawSql: SELECT bucket AS "time", sum(n) OVER (ORDER BY bucket) AS "total records" FROM (SELECT date_trunc('day', first_seen_at) AS bucket, count(*) AS n FROM bibliographic_records GROUP BY 1) s ORDER BY bucket
SELECT genre, count(*) AS n FROM bibliographic_records GROUP BY genre ORDER BY n DESC

panels.title: Availability snapshots per hour
panels.description: The 6-hourly refresh job's heartbeat.

targets.rawSql: SELECT date_trunc('hour', observed_at) AS "time", count(*) AS "snapshots" FROM availability_snapshots WHERE $__timeFilter(observed_at) GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1
SELECT titn, attempt_count, left(coalesce(last_error, ''), 80) AS last_error, last_attempted_at FROM scrape_tasks WHERE status = 'failed' ORDER BY last_attempted_at DESC NULLS LAST LIMIT 12
SELECT status, filename, total, inserted, updated, unmatched, created_at, finished_at FROM import_jobs ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 10

panels.title: Genre coverage over time (records with genre, by first_seen day)
panels.description: Approximation: groups current genre by ingest day — shows whether NEW records arrive classified (post-2026-06-12 crawler).

targets.rawSql: SELECT date_trunc('day', first_seen_at) AS "time", round(100.0 * count(*) FILTER (WHERE genre <> 'unknown') / greatest(count(*), 1), 1) AS "% known" FROM bibliographic_records WHERE $__timeFilter(first_seen_at) GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Relevance coverage
panels.description: Records with a relevance_score written by the nightly recompute (relevance_updated_at set). Sits near 100% once the 04:00 job has run.

targets.rawSql: SELECT round(100.0 * count(*) FILTER (WHERE relevance_updated_at IS NOT NULL) / greatest(count(*), 1), 1) AS pct FROM bibliographic_records

panels.title: Relevance freshness
panels.description: Hours since the most recent relevance recompute (now() − max(relevance_updated_at)). Nightly job runs 04:00 UTC, so this should stay under ~28h; >48h means a night was missed (crawler image not rebuilt, or the job failed).

targets.rawSql: SELECT round(extract(epoch FROM now() - max(relevance_updated_at)) / 3600.0, 1) AS hours FROM bibliographic_records

panels.title: Cold-start records
panels.description: Records with no availability history yet — demand set neutral (0.5) so new acquisitions aren't buried. Falls as the availability series grows.

targets.rawSql: SELECT count(*) AS cold_start FROM bibliographic_records WHERE (relevance_components->>'cold_start')::numeric = 1

panels.title: Mean relevance components
panels.description: Corpus-average of each [0,1] sub-score (demand · holdings · recency · completeness) from relevance_components — the shape of the blend across the mirror.

targets.rawSql: SELECT round(avg((relevance_components->>'demand')::numeric), 3) AS demand, round(avg((relevance_components->>'holdings')::numeric), 3) AS holdings, round(avg((relevance_components->>'recency')::numeric), 3) AS recency, round(avg((relevance_components->>'completeness')::numeric), 3) AS completeness FROM bibliographic_records WHERE relevance_components IS NOT NULL

panels.title: Canon seed size
panels.description: Total canonical works in canon_seed (Wikidata + curated award fallback). Grows on each monthly refresh-seed run (canon_seed job).

targets.rawSql: SELECT count(*) AS works FROM canon_seed

panels.title: Canon matched %
panels.description: Share of seed works the matcher linked to a record already in the mirror (matched_record_id set) — the classics the RBPA already holds, which the C2/C4 relevance boost lifts.

targets.rawSql: SELECT round(100.0 * count(*) FILTER (WHERE matched_record_id IS NOT NULL) / greatest(count(*), 1), 1) AS pct FROM canon_seed

panels.title: Canon held %
panels.description: Share of seed works confirmed held by the RBPA — matched in the mirror OR resolved as held/ingested via the OPAC (canon_resolve job). Grows as resolve runs.

targets.rawSql: SELECT round(100.0 * count(*) FILTER (WHERE matched_record_id IS NOT NULL OR acquire_status IN ('held','ingested')) / greatest(count(*), 1), 1) AS pct FROM canon_seed

panels.title: Canon acquire status
panels.description: canon_seed rows by acquire_status: unchecked (not yet resolved against the OPAC) · held · not_held · ingested. The canon_resolve job moves rows out of 'unchecked'.

targets.rawSql: SELECT count(*) FILTER (WHERE acquire_status = 'unchecked') AS unchecked, count(*) FILTER (WHERE acquire_status = 'held') AS held, count(*) FILTER (WHERE acquire_status = 'not_held') AS not_held, count(*) FILTER (WHERE acquire_status = 'ingested') AS ingested FROM canon_seed

panels.title: Canon OL ratings collected
panels.description: Seed works enriched with an Open Library rating count (ol_rating_count set) by enrich-ratings — the C4 popularity signal now feeding the canon relevance boost.

targets.rawSql: SELECT count(*) FILTER (WHERE ol_rating_count IS NOT NULL) AS rated FROM canon_seed

panels.title: Shelf unmatched
panels.description: User shelf entries not yet linked to a catalogue record (matched_record_id NULL). The demand-driven fetcher (shelf_resolve job) shrinks this by resolving them against the OPAC, then re-matching once the worker ingests.

targets.rawSql: SELECT count(*) AS unmatched FROM shelf_entries WHERE matched_record_id IS NULL

panels.title: Shelf matched %
panels.description: Share of all shelf entries linked to a catalogue record (cover + live availability shown on /shelf). Grows as the fetcher resolves held books and re-match links them.

targets.rawSql: SELECT round(100.0 * count(*) FILTER (WHERE matched_record_id IS NOT NULL) / greatest(count(*), 1), 1) AS pct FROM shelf_entries

panels.title: Shelf resolve status (unmatched)
panels.description: Still-unmatched shelf entries by resolve_status: unchecked (not yet asked the OPAC) · held (RBPA holds it, TITN seeded, awaiting ingest + re-match) · not_held (OPAC returned nothing; re-tried after the cooldown). The shelf_resolve job moves entries out of 'unchecked'.

targets.rawSql: SELECT count(*) FILTER (WHERE resolve_status = 'unchecked') AS unchecked, count(*) FILTER (WHERE resolve_status = 'held') AS held, count(*) FILTER (WHERE resolve_status = 'not_held') AS not_held FROM shelf_entries WHERE matched_record_id IS NULL

panels.title: Shelf held (seeded)
panels.description: Unmatched entries the fetcher confirmed the RBPA holds and seeded into scrape_tasks — awaiting the worker's ingest and the next `shelf rematch` to link them.

targets.rawSql: SELECT count(*) FILTER (WHERE resolve_status = 'held') AS held FROM shelf_entries WHERE matched_record_id IS NULL

panels.title: Shelf OPAC attempts
panels.description: Total OPAC resolve attempts across all shelf entries (sum of resolve_attempts) — the cumulative polite query budget the fetcher has spent on user shelves.

targets.rawSql: SELECT coalesce(sum(resolve_attempts), 0) AS attempts FROM shelf_entries
